paxton-ken.text: Ken Paxton (Republican) is a candidate for United States Senator from Texas.

On energy, Ken Paxton's recorded position: Paxton has strongly advocated for fossil fuel expansion, fighting to reinstate the Keystone XL pipeline, criticizing EPA rules targeting oil and gas, opposing FERC regulations on natural gas emissions, and arguing that 'green energy' cannot substitute for coal, nuclear, oil, and gas.
Ken Paxton's recorded background: Ken Paxton, attorney general of Texas (2015–present).
As of 2026-07-04, Ken Paxton's campaign had raised $7,605,209 in campaign receipts and spent $5,257,746, per FEC filings.

talarico-james.text: James Talarico (Democratic) is a candidate for United States Senator from Texas.

On taxes, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico explicitly calls for raising taxes on billionaires and corporations, including higher taxes on income and capital gains, ending 'buy, borrow, die' loopholes, raising corporate tax rates, and closing carried interest loopholes to fund working family tax cuts.
James Talarico's recorded background: James Talarico, state representative from the 50th district (2018–present).
As of 2026-07-04, James Talarico's campaign had raised $40,284,109 in campaign receipts and spent $30,425,244, per FEC filings.

base.summary: United States Senator from Texas race: Ken Paxton, James Talarico. This overview lists each candidate's party, background, and campaign finance where available in our data set.
base.caveats: Our data set for this race covers candidate name, party, incumbency, background summary, and campaign finance totals where a filing was found (federal candidates file with the FEC; Texas state offices file with the Texas Ethics Commission -- each finance bullet names which). We do not have recorded voting histories or issue-by-issue policy positions for these candidates in this data set.

now.text: On energy, Ken Paxton's recorded position is: "Paxton has strongly advocated for fossil fuel expansion, fighting to reinstate the Keystone XL pipeline, criticizing EPA rules targeting oil and gas, opposing FERC regulations on natural gas emissions, and arguing that 'green energy' cannot substitute for coal, nuclear, oil, and gas." That's where Ken Paxton stands right now, this term.

long_term.text: If Ken Paxton's recorded position on energy holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping this issue the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Ken Paxton keeps this position and it turns into lasting policy, rather than changing later or staying just talk.

talarico-james.text: On healthcare, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico supports allowing every American regardless of age to join Medicare as a public option, creating government-guaranteed coverage available to all while preserving private insurance competition. He has legislated to cap insulin costs and import cheaper drugs from Canada.
As of 2026-07-04, James Talarico's campaign had raised $40,284,109, per FEC filings -- fundraising scale only, not a stance on health coverage, the ACA, or Medicaid.

paxton-ken.text: No public data in our set ties Ken Paxton's background, votes, or stated positions to Medicare, Social Security, or retiree benefits.
As of 2026-07-04, Ken Paxton's campaign had raised $7,605,209, per FEC filings -- fundraising scale only, not a stance on Medicare, Social Security, or retiree benefits.

talarico-james.text: On social security, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico supports expanding Social Security benefits and funding them by eliminating the payroll tax cap on earnings over $400,000 annually. He opposes benefit cuts and privatization.

On medicare medicaid, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico supports expanding Medicare eligibility to all ages as a public option and protecting Medicaid from cuts. He has opposed legislation that would kick people off Medicaid coverage.
As of 2026-07-04, James Talarico's campaign had raised $40,284,109, per FEC filings -- fundraising scale only, not a stance on Medicare, Social Security, or retiree benefits.
